guideline_references: The Ehlers-Danlos Society: 2017 International Classification of the Ehlers-Danlos Syndromes (hypermobile EDS diagnostic criteria and management framework)
RACGP: management of chronic musculoskeletal pain and joint hypermobility (general practice guidance)
intentional_test_features: Over-triage resistance: an alarming presentation (severe pain, joints 'popping out', immobilised on the floor, tachycardia) in a KNOWN chronic condition (hypermobile EDS) whose acute red-flag screen is NEGATIVE (joints spontaneously reduced, distal neurovascular intact, afebrile). The correct answer is to recognise the flare, tier it as prompt-primary-care with prominent emergency safety-netting, and NOT default to ED/ambulance.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is an in-person clinician who examined the joints, checked distal pulses/neurology, scored Beighton, and administered oral diazepam. The AI Doctor cannot examine or confirm reduction and is scored on recognise + arrange in-person confirmation/support + safety-net.
Two-sided diagnostic trap: visceral/framing bias toward over-escalation, versus premature-closure toward 'just a flare' that would miss a genuine unreduced dislocation or neurovascular compromise.
Welfare dimension: immobilised patient living alone, unable to reach water — a same-day support need distinct from a medical emergency.
Source category: CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities), source case CFE-020.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
